description: CS learning coach eval baseline — validates teaching quality across computer science sub-domains

evals.name: algorithm-two-sum
evals.prompt: I want to learn the two-sum algorithm problem, LeetCode #1. I have 15 minutes and want a guided walkthrough.
evals.expected_output: Should route to Mode A (Algorithm Visualization), including: initial intuition, concrete hand-trace, variable list, code mapping, self-check list. Should NOT directly provide the complete solution code.
assertions: routes to algorithm visualization mode
includes a concrete hand-trace with a small example
includes variable list in table format
includes self-check list
does not directly give the complete code solution

evals.name: socratic-redis-interview
evals.prompt: Help me prepare for a Redis persistence interview question. I know there's RDB and AOF, but I'm fuzzy on the details.
evals.expected_output: Should route to Mode B (Socratic + Advanced Recall), first probe what the user already knows, then guide the user to recall and derive through a question chain — not explain directly.
assertions: routes to socratic mode
asks about the user's existing knowledge before explaining
designs a progressive question chain
includes hints about interviewer follow-up depth
ends with a list of weak spots

evals.name: mental-model-transformer
evals.prompt: I want to understand the self-attention mechanism in Transformers. It feels very abstract — can you draw a diagram to help me understand?
evals.expected_output: Should route to Mode C (Mental Model + Dual Coding), including ASCII diagrams or structured visual representation alongside text explanation.
assertions: routes to mental model + dual coding mode
includes visual representation (ASCII diagram / table / flowchart)
dual explanation: text and visual
includes connection test questions
first asks about the user's existing understanding

evals.name: knowledge-source-with-code
evals.prompt: I've uploaded a set of Go concurrency notes (assume provided). Help me review channel usage based on these notes.
evals.expected_output: Should prioritize teaching from the user-provided notes, citing specific content from the notes — not substituting with the model's own knowledge.
assertions: explicitly references the user-provided materials
teaching content based on user materials, not model knowledge
cites knowledge source
mentions will supplement with search if materials are insufficient

evals.name: systematic-system-design
evals.prompt: I want to systematically study distributed system design from scratch. Give me a 30-minute study plan.
evals.expected_output: Should route to Mode F (Interleaved Practice + Generative Learning), design a mixed study plan with interleaved practice across different distributed concepts.
assertions: routes to interleaved practice + generative learning mode
designs a 30-minute study plan
includes mixed practice problems
includes generative tasks
probes the user's current level
